pages.raw_text:
Secretary Charles Perry Taylor
of the Washington State Fedora.
ton of Labor has issued the fol-
lowing greeting to all affiliated
unions:


within one week the date will
have arrived for the eighth annual
convention of the Washington
State Federation of Labor, which
meets at Y. M. c. A. hall, Walla
Walla, aJnuary Z, at 10 a. m. The
number of duplicate credentials
received at headquarters indicates
that the session will be largely at
tended by a most representative
body of active workers in labor's
cause.


"The growth of the organization
during the quarter just closed has
been very marked. The following
organizations have affiliated since
last report: Steam Engineers,
Sheet Metal Workers, Bartenders,
Everett; Garment Workers, Horse
shoers, Retail Clerks, Seattle;
Single Weavers, Steam Engi-
neers. Olympia; Printing Press
men, Dillingham;; Bartenders, Fed
eral Labor Union, Transfers, Spo-
kane; Mine Workers, Ravensdale;
Painters, Sheet Metal Workers,
Woman's Label League, Tacoma;
Building Laborers, Georgetown.
The Woman's Label League. of Sc
attic, has withdrawn.


''Since the last quarterly letter
was issued, our able leaders in the
